prompt:
A deep rectangular hydroponic starter tub about 6–8 inches (15–20 cm) deep, filled with expanded clay pebbles (Hydroton) and dedicated to young stevia plants just establishing themselves. Several small but leafy stevia starts grow from individual net pots or planting sites spaced along the tub, their stems straight and fresh with compact crowns of leaves. The depth of the tub and thick layer of pebbles make it clear there is plenty of room below the surface for the developing root systems, signaling a serious hydroponic setup rather than a shallow decorative planter.

Near-future solarpunk interior with soft, even lighting and light-colored shelving or countertops, with a few other potted plants in the background. A small, clearly visible but text-free nutrient bottle and a measuring cup sit beside the tub to hint at careful preparation. A subtle smart sensor or tiny display on the tub shows simple icons (for example, leaf, water drop, light) indicating healthy conditions without any readable text. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos, and no people in frame—the focus is entirely on the deep hydroponic tub and the promising young stevia plants at the beginning of their growth cycle.
